threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think ‘the Lido’ means just one place, not the half-dozen spots that stir arguments on sunny days.
Caught a corner of the 59 bus last night; a chap was serenading a sleeping lady with opera. Only in Brixton would this be a Tuesday night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I stepped over a random pile of discarded furniture and saw a family of raccoons moving in like they owned the place.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice Berliners will carefully place their trash to balance on top of each other instead of just using the bin? It's a whole art form.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their love for that hidden taco truck on 24th—it’s not on Google Maps, just a tip from a true Missionite.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus with a saxophonist belting out Coltrane. Totally out of place, yet perfectly San Francisco. Only here, folks!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think they can just grab a coffee at any plaza, but only locals know which cafés serve the real deal. It’s a taste test!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newbie by their bewildered look when the metro stops unexpectedly. That Sagrada Família to La Pau stretch can throw anyone off.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newcomer by their confusion at the bus routes – they still look at a map instead of knowing to just hop on any 149.
Just spotted a guy in a crocodile onesie on the 59 last night—he started singing old soul tracks. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a kid riding their bike on the sidewalk while their grandma shouted at a pigeon. That's Berlin in a nutshell.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you catch a spontaneous outdoor rave under an overpass after dark. The bass reverberates, and it feels like a secret just for us.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local if they can name at least three taco trucks with their salsas and still have a favorite burrito spot that’s not Taqueria Cancun.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus full of parents and toddlers singing 'Baby Shark' at the top of their lungs. Only in the Mission, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often forget that the local bakeries close by 8 PM—no fresh bread after dinner, just stale regrets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them at the Sagrada Família staring up with wide eyes, still fumbling with their metro maps and wondering why the L2 is down.
